data.title: Disney beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q3 and updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Disney press release (NYSE:DIS): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.45 beats by $0.24.
  • Revenue of $23.6B (+6.8% Y/Y) beats by $470M.
  • Direct-to-Consumer operating income increased $289 million to $336 million
  • 180.7 million Disney+ and Hulu subscriptions, an increase of 2.5 million versus Q1 fiscal 2025
  • 126.0 million Disney+ subscribers, an increase of 1.4 million versus Q1 fiscal 2025
  • Linear Networks operating income grew 2%; year-over-year growth includes a comparison to $89 million of operating income in Q2 fiscal 2024 from Star India

  • Guidance and Outlook:

    • Q3 Fiscal 2025:
      • Entertainment Direct-to-Consumer: Modest increase in Disney+ subscribers compared to Q2 fiscal 2025
    • Fiscal Year 2025:
      • Adjusted EPS(1) of $5.75, an increase of 16% over fiscal 2024 vs. consnesus of $5.44 (prior High-single digit adjusted EPS growth compared to fiscal 2024)
      • Cash provided by operations of $17 billion, a $2 billion increase over prior guidance driven by the deferral of tax payments
      • Entertainment: Double-digit percentage segment operating income growth
      • Sports: 18% segment operating income growth
      • Experiences: 6% to 8% segment operating income growth
      • Disney Cruise Line pre-opening expense of ~$200 million, with ~$40 million in Q3 and ~$50 million in Q4
      • Equity loss from India JV of ~$300 million driven by purchase accounting amortization

data.title: The ODP Corporation Non-GAAP EPS of $1.06 beats by $0.41, revenue of $1.7B beats by $30M
data.description:
• The ODP Corporation press release (NASDAQ:ODP): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.06 beats by $0.41.
  • Revenue of $1.7B (-10.5% Y/Y) beats by $30M.

  • GAAP Operating Loss of $32 Million; Net Loss of $29 Million; Operating Cash Flow of $57 Million

    Adjusted EBITDA of $76 Million; Adjusted Free Cash Flow of $45 Million

data.title: CrowdStrike to cut nearly 500 jobs, reaffirms FY26 outlook - report
data.description:
CrowdStrike (NASDAQ:CRWD) plans to cut about 500 jobs, or about 5% of its global workforce, as part of the company's efforts to evolve its operations and “yield greater efficiencies," Bloomberg News reported.

The company expects to continue to hire in key strategic areas throughout its fiscal year, the report added.

CrowdStrike did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

CrowdStrike noted that it sees first quarter results to be in-line with or above its guidance provided during its fourth quarter results on March 4.

The cybersecurity provider also reaffirmed its guidance for the fiscal year 2026.

CrowdStrike expects about $36M to $53M in charges related to the job cuts.

“Our offices will be closed May 7 and 8, so please plan to work from home," CEO George Kurtz said in memo to the staff, the report noted.

data.title: Turning Points Brands tops estimates after the Stoker's brand outperforms
data.description:
Turning Point Brands (NYSE:TPB) reported that total consolidated net sales increased 28.1% in Q1 to $106.4 million, driven higher by a 62.7% pop in Stoker's Products net sales.

The Stoker's Products growth was driven by a strong performance for Modern Oral sales, low double-digit growth in MST, and low single-digit growth in looseleaf. For the quarter, total Stoker's Products segment volume increased 55.1%, while price/product mix increased 7.6%.

Gross profit increased 23.3% year-over-year to $59.6 million during Q1. Net income gained 19.8% to $14.4 million. Adjusted EBITDA rose 12.0% to $27.7 million. EPS was reported at $0.91 vs. $0.78 consensus and $0.80 a year ago.

"We are pleased with our first quarter results. Modern Oral sales were $22.3 million, up nearly 10-times versus the prior year and nearly double the prior quarter. Stoker’s MST and looseleaf exceeded our expectations, and Zig-Zag was in line with our expectations," stated CEO Graham Purdy.

Looking ahead, the Louisville-based company maintained its previous expectation for full-year 2025 adjusted EBITDA of $108.0 to $113.0 million. 

Shares of Turning Point Brands (NYSE:TPB) edged 0.2% higher in premarket trading. The consumer products stock is up 8.0% on a year-to-date basis. Short interest on TPB is 4.8% of the total float.

data.title: Azenta Non-GAAP EPS of $0.05, revenue of $143M
data.description:
• Azenta press release (NASDAQ:AZTA): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.05.
  • Revenue of $143M (-3.4% Y/Y).
  • The Company is reiterating its revenue guidance for fiscal year 2025:
    • Total organic revenue is expected to grow in the range of 3% to 5% relative to fiscal 2024.
    • Adjusted EBITDA margin expansion is expected to be approximately 300 basis points relative to fiscal 2024.

data.title: AvidXchange misses top-line and bottom-line estimates; suspends FY25 outlook
data.description:
• AvidXchange press release (NASDAQ:AVDX): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.05 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $107.94M (+2.2% Y/Y) misses by $1.7M.
  • Q1'25 versus Q1'24 net cash provided by operating activities swings to $7.4 million from a use of ($6.4) million in the year ago period
  • The Company sustains strong balance sheet with cash and marketable securities of $389.3 million

  • Full Year 2025 Financial Outlook

    AvidXchange is suspending its previously issued financial outlook for fiscal 2025 in light of its pending acquisition by TPG in partnership with Corpay.

data.title: Radware looks to expand market presence
data.description:
• Radware (NASDAQ:RDWR) said it is looking to expand its market presence and boost growth across its cloud services business in the U.S. 
  • The firm said it is making new hires, adding tech alliances, and reinforcing its commitment to AI innovation. 
  • The company's plans come after the firm delivered a beat to its top and bottom line estimates earlier in the day.

data.title: BorgWarner beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• BorgWarner press release (NYSE:BWA): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.11 beats by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $3.52B (-2.2% Y/Y) beats by $120M.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities of $82 million.
    • Free cash flow of $(35) million.

    • Full Year 2025 Guidance Update: The Company has provided a 2025 full year guidance update. Net sales are expected to be in the range of $13.6 billion to $14.2 billion in 2025, compared with 2024 sales of approximately $14.1 billion. This is a slight increase from the Company's previous range as a result of favorable impacts from foreign exchange and tariff customer recoveries which were mostly offset by lower industry production expectations. The Company expects its weighted light and commercial vehicle markets to be in the range of down 4% to down 2% in 2025. This is a reduction from the Company's prior range of down 3% to down 1% primarily due to the potential impact of tariffs on global industry production. The Company's sales guidance implies a year-over-year change in organic sales of down 2% to up 2% or estimated outgrowth above market production of approximately 200 to 400 basis points. The increase in the Company's estimated outgrowth compared to its previous guidance of 100 to 300 basis points reflects anticipated tariff customer recoveries. Stronger foreign currencies are expected to result in an increase in sales of $250 million compared to the Company's previous guidance.

      The Company expects operating margin to be in the range of 8.1% to 8.6% in 2025. Excluding the impact of non-comparable items and the add back of intangible asset amortization expense, adjusted operating margin is expected to be in the range of 9.6% to 10.2%, which includes a 20 basis point dilutive impact from anticipated tariff customer recoveries. The change compared to the Company's previous adjusted operating margin range of 10.0% to 10.2% is due to expected lower industry production and the dilutive impact of anticipated tariff customer recoveries on the Company's margin profile. Net earnings are expected to be within the range of $3.44 to $3.85 per diluted share. Excluding the impact of non-comparable items, adjusted net earnings are expected to be in the range of $4.00 to $4.45 per diluted share vs. $4.12 consensus (prior $4.05 to $4.40), compared to the Company's previous adjusted net earnings range of $4.05 to $4.40 per diluted share. Full-year operating cash flow is expected to be unchanged and in the range of $1,323 million to $1,375 million, and free cash flow is expected to be unchanged and in the range of $650 million to $750 million.

data.title: Kennametal reports mixed Q3 results; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Kennametal press release (NYSE:KMT): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.47 beats by $0.23.
  • Revenue of $486M (-5.8% Y/Y) misses by $3.3M.
  • The adjusted ETR was 22.8 percent in the current quarter, compared to 26.5 percent in the prior year quarter.
  • Year-to-date net cash flow from operating activities was $130 million compared to $163 million in the prior year period. 

  • Outlook

    The Company's expectations for the full fiscal year 2025 are as follows:

    Annual Outlook:

    • Sales now expected to be $1.970 - $1.990 billion vs. $1.97B consensus (prior $1.950 - $2.0 billion)
    • Adjusted EPS is now expected to be $1.30 - $1.45 vs. $1.13 consensus (prior $1.05 - $1.30)
    • Pricing actions expected to cover raw material costs, wages and general inflation
    • Interest expense is expected to be approximately $27 million
    • Adjusted ETR is now expected to be approximately 25 percent
    • Free operating cash flow of greater than 125 percent of adjusted net income
    • Primary working capital as a percent of sales is now expected to be approximately 32 percent by fiscal year-end
    • Capital spending now expected to be approximately $90 million

data.title: India launches airstrikes on Pakistan, world leaders call for restraint
data.description:
India has launched airstrikes on nine sites that it described as "terrorist infrastructure" in Pakistan and Pakistan-administered Kashmir, in response to last month's terror attack in Pahalgam.

Pakistan said India's attacks have killed at least 26 people, while Indian officials said at least 10 people were killed in India-administered Kashmir due to Pakistani fire. Pakistan also said it shot down five Indian fighter jets, including a French-made Rafale, a claim that India has not addressed yet.

India said its actions were "focused, measured and non-escalatory in nature." Pakistan said it "reserves the right to respond, in self-defense, at a time, place, and manner of its choosing."

Several countries, including China and Russia, have called on both countries to exercise restraint and avoid escalation into a full-blown military conflict. "I hope it ends very quickly," U.S. President Donald Trump said.

India said the Pahalgam attack, which killed 26 tourists in April, was planned from Pakistan. While Islamabad repeatedly denied any involvement, the attack led to a breakdown in already-shaky diplomatic ties between the two nuclear-armed countries.

India's benchmark stock indices (NIFTY) (SENSEX) ended Wednesday's volatile session marginally higher.  Pakistan's Karachi 100 index sank 5.5% in early trade, before paring losses to end 3% lower.

Indian defense stocks rose in early trade on Wednesday, after adding over $5B in market value since the Pahalgam attack, Bloomberg reported. Chinese defense firms, which supply Pakistan, have also gained, with the CSI Defense Index up 4% on Wednesday.
